title: Korean Kimchi Fried Rice

subs.name: 150 g shredded cabbage sautéed with 1 tbsp red chilli sauce and 1 tsp vinegar for 5 minutes
subs.notes: quick stand-in when fermented kimchi is unavailable

subs.name: crumbled firm tofu cooked with a pinch of turmeric for a vegan version
sambal oelek or sriracha with a pinch of sugar — use the same quantity

steps.text: Separate the spring onion whites from the greens. Finely mince the whites and slice the greens on a diagonal for garnish. Break the cold cooked rice apart with your hands so there are no large clumps — loose grains fry better.
Heat the cooking oil and gingelly oil together in a kadai or wok over high flame until the oils shimmer. Add the spring onion whites and minced garlic and stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the chopped kimchi and stir-fry on high heat for 2 minutes until it darkens slightly and the sharp raw smell mellows. The kimchi should sizzle loudly when it hits the pan.

Stir in the gochujang and soy sauce to coat the kimchi, then tip in the cold rice. Press and toss the rice briskly until every grain is coated in the brick-red sauce, about 3 minutes. Keep the heat high throughout so the rice fries rather than steams.
Push the rice to the sides of the pan, crack the eggs into the centre and scramble them until just set, about 60 seconds. Fold the egg into the rice and stir everything together.
Taste for salt — the kimchi and soy sauce are already salty, so go carefully. Scatter sesame seeds and spring onion greens over the top and serve immediately from the pan.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: kimchi → 150 g shredded cabbage sautéed with 1 tbsp red chilli sauce and 1 tsp vinegar for 5 minutes (quick stand-in when fermented kimchi is unavailable); gochujang → sambal oelek or sriracha with a pinch of sugar — use the same quantity; egg → crumbled firm tofu cooked with a pinch of turmeric for a vegan version
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Kimchi fried rice is perfectly suited to an Indian kitchen — use a kadai on high flame in place of a wok, keeping the heat intense throughout so the rice fries rather than steams. Gochujang is now stocked in specialty sections of supermarkets in Indian cities; failing that, mix 1 tbsp red chilli paste with 1 tsp soy sauce as a rough substitute. Gingelly oil (nallennai in South Indian stores, til ka tel in North India) adds the characteristic nutty depth. Use leftover refrigerator-cold rice — freshly cooked rice turns mushy.
